Lead partner has put much effort into establishing efficient communication between the project partners as well as between the consortium and Joint Secretariat of the Interreg-Euro-Med programme. There is a constant exchange of information via email, phone call or ZOOM meetings in order to ensure progress and coordination of all activities. 
The vast majority of the partners have performed their administrative and financial duties and submitted partner reports on time. Accordingly, they received Certificates from their national controllers.
For promotional and visibility purposes, FRED is promoted during various activities and happenings. The official website of the project has been approved and published (web page link: https://fred.interreg-euro-med.eu/ ). Social media channels – Facebook, Instagram and Linkedin are also established and properly updated with news and general information of the project. All partners are encouraged to publish news on their official websites and social media channels. LP also participated in the event organized by Natural Heritage Mission in Rovinj (26-27.6.2024), where we shared our goals, ideas and future steps with other Thematic project representatives.The official version of the poster for FRED project was sent to all partners so that each of them hangs a poster in their own premises.
Carbon footprint is monitored per each project partner and needed documents are uploaded on the ZeroCO2MED platform as well. 
About particular work package activities project partners actively participated and performed their duties timely. As part of WP1, Activity 1.1., deliverable D1.1.3 "Capitalization paper – lessons learnt from past projects" is finalized. In summary, capitalizing on previous results within an experienced and multidisciplinary consortium will lead to greater efficiency, quality, risk management, competitiveness and innovation, ultimately enhancing FRED’s overall success and impact.
With reference to the activities and deliverables planned in the next period, all partners are collaborating together with the responsible and supporting partners on preparatory activities to the creation of “D1.1.1 Preliminary data collection and processing study” and “D1.1.2 Pilot Case Implementation Methodology paper”.
Within the Activity 1.2 Specification and implementation of the Wildfire risk prevention and mitigation platform, deliverable D.1.2.1 Functional design of Wildfire risk prevention and mitigation platform has been created. The work on the specification document includes description of user stories, a tool used in agile development methodologies to capture a description of a software feature from an end-user perspective.
With regard to the WP2, Activity 2.3 Carbon footprint monitoring, all partners have uploaded on the Basecamp their carbon footprint in accordance with the provided table.
Deliverable  D4.1.1  Stakeholders and beneficiaries’ database, a deliverable has been created within WP4. The partners were instructed to populate the database with their relevant stakeholders. The total of over 200 entries has been reached, with more entries to come as we continue to implement the project. It is expected to be updated throughout project lifetime. 
With regard to the activity 4.3 Coordination with the TCP and IDP, partners participated in webinars organized by Natural Heritage mission, in order to ignite collaboration, foster capacity building, and share valuable knowledge on crucial policy matters. Also, LP participated in the event organized by Natural Heritage Mission in Rovinj, where we shared our goals and discussed about future steps with other Thematic project representatives. All deliverables were produced on time as foreseen in the AF.
Some partners have already started activities for the purchase of equipment and procurement procedures. It is expected that in the next period those processes will be finished.

All project partners continued to work efficiently, meeting their deadlines and fulfilling their duties as planned. Each partner successfully submitted their partner reports and obtained Certificates from their national controllers, ensuring compliance with administrative and financial regulations. Only Certificate for PP4 is missing. The Lead Partner maintained strong communication with all project partners and the Joint Secretariat. Regular exchanges of information were facilitated through emails, phone calls, and online meetings to ensure smooth progress and coordination of activities. To ensure alignment and successful execution, several online meetings were conducted with project partners, facilitating discussions on pilot site descriptions and implementation scenarios. 
The second Project Consortium and third Steering Committee meeting took place on November 13–14, 2024, in Potenza, Italy. The majority of project partners attended in person, presenting draft deliverables completed so far. Partners unable to travel, participated online, along with the JS officer, Ms. Agathe Spitz. CGIAM – host of the meeting, organized study visits at the Control Rooms of Basilicata Region Civil Protection and Municipality of Potenza - Office of Civil Protection. These achievements reflect the project’s continued commitment to efficiency, collaboration, and progress toward its objectives.
The project made significant progress in the WP1 Defining and implementing tools for wildfire prevention and mitigation. A major focus was placed on two key deliverables:
-	D1.1.1 Preliminary Data Collection and Processing Study: This study integrated methodologies, tools, and insights from previous projects to enhance fire risk assessment and prevention strategies. The document covers key aspects such as fuel mapping, image analysis, and meteorological data utilization to improve wildfire prediction models.
-	D1.1.2 Pilot Case Implementation Methodology Paper: This deliverable provided a comprehensive methodology for pilot case implementation. The paper covers system design, UAV configurations, pilot site descriptions, and specific use cases in fire prevention, firefighting logistics, and post-event damage assessment.
Within the activity 1.1 Implementation Methodology, deliverable D1.1.3 Capitalization paper was finalized at the end of the first period.
The team contributed to the D1.2.1 Functional design and initial development of the Wildfire Risk Prevention and Mitigation platform. The platform aims to integrate multiple wildfire risk assessment tools, providing real-time analysis and decision-making support. Key contributions include fire simulation and risk assessment integration, data collection and processing and stakeholder engagement.
A crucial activity for the execution of six pilot actions is the A1.3 Procurement of UAVs and auxiliary equipment. The procurement process was completed successfully for the LP, with all necessary equipment delivered and deployed for field activities. Other pilot partners were in a process of preparing the tender or starting the procurement process. 
LP has already started recording wildfire-prone locations in Mali Lošinj and Cres, enhancing data collection for risk assessment and mitigation strategies. The use of UAV technology will continue in the next phase, with additional pilot areas expected to be surveyed.
Carbon footprint is a part of WP2 Piloting tools for WFPM within the Activity A2.3 Carbon footprint monitoring. Carbon footprint is monitored in each period and needed documents are uploaded on the ZeroCO2MED platform as well. 
As part of WP4 Contribution to the Results Amplification Strategy, a comprehensive database was developed in the first period, listing key stakeholders, including fire brigades, municipal authorities, civil protection agencies, and research institutions as D.4.1.1 Stakeholders and Beneficiaries Database. The table is being updated on regular basis. Stakeholders have been provided with the first FRED newsletter. FRED team members participated on webinars organized by Natural Heritage Mission such as Working group webinar and Joint communication meeting. RGO had a representative present on the event "2nd Mission for Natural Heritage Institutional Dialogue" in Malaga, 28 and 29 November 2024, where FRED project was presented in multiple networking sessions and graphical presentation to the participants, as well as online.
For promotional and visibility purposes, FRED is promoted during various activities and happenings such as international conference SUTRA and during the European Week of Regions and Cities, using a PechaKucha format to share knowledge with other regions. Official website of the project and social media channels – Facebook, Instagram and Linkedin are properly updated and shared with the public. During this second period, first Newsletter was published. Partners are encouraged to disseminate the project on their official websites.
During the third reporting period, the FRED project achieved significant progress across all work packages, consolidating results from previous periods and ensuring effective coordination among partners. All partners continued to meet deadlines and fulfil their responsibilities in line with the work plan. Partner reports were submitted on time and national controllers issued the necessary certificates, with the exception of PP4. The Lead Partner maintained efficient communication with both the partnership and the Joint Secretariat, ensuring that information flow, administrative follow-up and financial reporting were managed without delays. 
The third Project Consortium Meeting and the fourth Steering Committee Meeting were successfully organized in May 2025 in Sežana, Slovenia, hosted by the Fire and Rescue Service of Sežana with coordination support from PFRI. These meetings enabled in-depth discussions on project progress, financial management, pilot activity planning and forthcoming deliverables. In parallel, a comprehensive training session on unmanned aerial systems (UAS) was conducted, offering participants both theoretical knowledge and hands-on practice with drones and related equipment such as thermal cameras, LiDAR and radar sensors. The training was highly valued, as it directly strengthened the operational capacity of technical staff and pilot partners.
Furthermore, activities related to LiDAR mapping and data processing advanced significantly during the reporting period. Data collection and processing were successfully completed for three pilot areas in Croatia, Slovenia and BIH. 
Considerable effort was invested in the continued development of the FRED platform, the project’s core output. Within WP1, the implementation methodology was finalized and submitted, while the functional design of the platform (deliverable D1.2.1) was completed with contributions from multiple partners. Software development advanced steadily, covering coding, GIS data integration and interface design, while adjustments were continuously made to reflect feedback from pilot partners and lessons learned from fieldwork. Several scientific and technical partners made crucial contributions.
Parallel to software development, procurement of UAVs and auxiliary equipment advanced considerably. Several partners, including PFRI, NPUNA, CIMBAL and FRSS, successfully completed procurement procedures and received advanced drone systems.Training activities ensured that pilots and operators became proficient in using the equipment, integrating it with the platform and applying it in complex field conditions.
Within WP2, partners worked on training materials, reporting obligations and the systematic monitoring of the project’s carbon footprint. Data were collected and uploaded to the ZeroCO2MED platform, contributing to the deliverable on project-wide carbon footprint calculation. In WP3, DUTH initiated the development of the evaluation methodology, identifying 18 use cases for pilot sites and defining preliminary indicators to assess the efficiency and effectiveness of the platform. This preparatory work ensures that testing in the next phases will be measurable, structured and aligned with user needs.
Project partners also invested significant efforts in dissemination, visibility and stakeholder engagement. A professionally produced project video was approved by the Joint Secretariat’s communication team and widely shared on social media and among stakeholders. The project was presented at several events, including Faculty Career Day in Croatia and a local TV broadcast in Montenegro, raising awareness among students, professionals and the wider public. Official project channels on Facebook, Instagram and LinkedIn were regularly updated with project news. Partners actively participated in communication and networking activities under the Interreg Euro-MED Natural Heritage Mission, attending webinars, cluster initiatives and peer review exercises, thereby amplifying the reach and visibility of FRED beyond the immediate consortium.
A dedicated stakeholders and beneficiaries database (deliverable 4.1.1) was created and regularly updated, strengthening cooperation with municipalities, fire brigades, national parks and civil protection authorities. 
In conclusion, the third reporting period marked a phase of consolidation and expansion for the FRED project. The consortium successfully combined effective management and communication with tangible technical progress on the platform, substantial investments in UAV procurement and training, the establishment of evaluation mechanisms, and wide-ranging dissemination efforts. Through these coordinated achievements, the project strengthened its capacity to deliver innovative tools for wildfire risk prevention and mitigation, while fostering strong collaboration among Mediterranean partners and stakeholders.

en.name: A multitude of inclusive Renewable Energy Communities in the Mediterranean Region
en.description:
A clean and just energy transition can only rely on the direct involvement of all the actors operating in the territories: citizens, public authorities, private enterprises and scientific institutions.
A stronger cooperation on a transnational level is required to enhance an integrated approach to innovate current models on the basis of the best practices and studies performed in different areas so far.
RECinMED, through a partnership composed by 11 project partners coming from 9 countries and three associated partners, aims at testing innovative solutions in order to foster the creation and good functioning of inclusive Renewable Energy Communities.
The project will develop a new cooperation forum and coordination activities on a transnational level to strengthen the networks and positive spillovers among projects regarding energy communities.
In addition, three Working Groups will be established in order to analyze existing knowledge, design and implement pilot actions in three different contexts: urban areas, rural territories and islands.
Each WG will face specific challenges and will be able to identify the best way to transfer and upscale validated solutions in new areas.
Methodologies and tools already developed in previous EU projects have been selected. They will be analyzed and integrated according to best practices and available real-data to make them more effective and transferable in geographical contexts with similar traits and necessities. 
Testing reports, stakeholder engagement initiatives and a Transferability Plan will be delivered together with an ongoing quality assessment both for technical actions and project management, including communication.
en.expected_achievements:
Facilitate transnational cooperation among public and private stakeholders involved in the development of Renewable Energy Communities.

This WP will be coordinated by the LP, School Center Velenje
Test methodologies and tools for the development of RECs in different scenarios on a transnational scale

This WP will be coordinated by the PP2, University of Bologna
Provide validated information on the efficacy of methodologies, support schemes and tools for the development of RECs in Mediterranean islands, urban and rural areas, while defining a Transferability plan

This WP will be coordinated by the PP9, Neapolis University Pafos
en.actual_achievements:
The pre-contracting negotiation phase took a particularly long time and ended with the signing of the Partnership Agreement, at the end of March, and the signing of the Subsidy Contract, in mid-April.

In the meantime, the project had begun its activities, with the Kick Off Meeting held online on 4 March 2024 (Minutes attached), and a second technical meeting, also held online, on 21 March 2024 (Minutes attached).
During the KOM, the Steering Committee was set up (Minutes attached).

On 4 and 5 June, the first F2F Meeting was held in Granada, Spain (Minutes attached) and also the 2nd Steering Committee Meeting (Minutes attached).
The Quality Plan and Communication Plan were also presented during this meeting. (both attached)

During the Granada Meeting, the 3 Working Groups met for the first time and started working. The report of the work done is contained in the Minutes of the Meeting itself.

On 10 April, the Kick Off Meeting of the Green Living Areas Community took place in Turin, Italy. For RECinMED, Igor Doler (LP) and Marco Affronte (PM) were present.

Marco Affronte (PM) and Francesco Suzzi (PP6) participated to the online GLA's Thematic Working Groups 
focusing on policy, on June 11th.

8 PPs out of 11, hung posters of the project in their offices, and uploaded photos to Basecamp. Attached photos.
Although the official project start date remained at 1 January 2024, the project really got underway with the technical work at the end of the negotiation and fulfilment of the pre-project tasks (Subsidy Contract, Partnership Agreement).  The technical specifications of WP1 were first entered into at the meeting on 21 March. Then, in Granada, the work of the WGs started concretely and continued until the end of the year (and beyond) with the analysis of the previous projects and solutions already developed (A 1.3) and contact with the stakeholders to be involved in the 3 WGs. Of course all the PPs were involved in this activities.
At the same time, constant contact and coordination with Renewable Energy Thematic Community, IDP and JS (A1.1) was maintained.
At the end of the year, the Pilot Transnational Cooperation Forum for the development of RECs was activated (A1.2). Activity 1.4, namely the design of the 3 Pilot Actions, builds on the work done in this second semester, in particular that of A1.3, and will be developed in the following months.
During this first year, the project's Editorial Communication Plan was also drawn up by the LP, as well as all the material for the visual identity. Likewise, the RECinMED Project Carbon Footprint Reduction Strategy was prepared and the first Project Carbon Footprint_Report; both of them by the responsible Partner, PP10.
The LP was also involved in the development of the Quality Toolkit, which was shared and elaborated in consultation with the entire Consortium.

During the first year the following meetings were held (Minutes attached in the Report Annexes section):
- 4.3.2024 - KOM (online) + 1st Steering Committee Meeting
- 21.3.2024 - Technical Project Meeting (online)
- 4/5.6.2024 - 3rd Project Meeting (1st F2F) in Granada, Spain + 2nd Steering Committee Meeting
- 3.7.2024 Capacity building meeting (online)
- 27/28.11.2024 - 4th Project Meeting (2nd F2F) in Pafos, Cyprus + 3rd Steering Committee Meeting
As in the first year of the project, constant contact and coordination with the Renewable Energy Thematic Community, IDP, and JS (A1.1) was also maintained during Period 3. Members of RECinMED’s partnership participated in three events in the first semester of 2025:
- Green Living Area Online Thematic Working Groups on policy (27.02.2025, online)
- Clean Energy for EU Islands Forum (14–15.05.2025, in person)
- 2nd Gathering of the Green Living Areas Thematic Community, Barcelona (28–29.05.2025, in person)

The Transnational Cooperation Forum for the development of RECs (A1.2), launched in Period 2, continued to engage both the community and the public interested in RECs, including through the implementation of an editorial plan whereby partners take turns posting topics and news on the Forum to stimulate comments and debate.

For Activities 1.3 and 1.4, the three WGs, after analysing in detail previous projects and already developed solutions, and after discussion and identification of the three potential Pilot Actions to be implemented in the study areas (urban, rural, and islands), developed the three Action Plans, which together also constitute Output 1.2.

Regarding Deliverable 1.4.1: during the meetings of 23.04.2025 (online) and 27–28.05.2025 (in person), there was extensive discussion on how to organise the work for the training course. In Smolyan, the final structure was agreed (six modules), and the partners responsible for developing them were assigned. The LP will then compile the six modules into a single final product. This work was not completed in Period 3 but will be finalised in Period 4.

For Activity 1.6, during the Smolyan Meeting in May 2025, the Technical Qualitative Assessment was carried out. The assessment focused on analysing the effectiveness of the three Action Plans delivered by the project partners, grouped into three clusters, each addressing a Pilot Action. A report was produced.

During the third semester, the following meetings were held (minutes attached in the Report Annexes):

18.02.2025 – 5th Project Meeting (online) + 4th Steering Committee Meeting
27–28.05.2025 – 6th Project Meeting (3rd face-to-face) in Smolyan, Bulgaria + 5th Steering Committee Meeting

- Communication -
RECinMED actively strengthened its dissemination efforts during Period 3:
The project’s social media presence grew steadily: the Facebook page reached 54 likes (+3 compared to P2) and 81 followers (+8 compared to P2), while the LinkedIn page achieved 108 followers (+32 compared to P2). All partners contributed to dissemination through their institutional channels, enhancing project visibility.

The “Euro-MED Transnational Cooperation” Forum was launched on LinkedIn in January 2025 and arrive to 70 members at the end of Period 3. Each partner is responsible for leading a monthly thematic discussion and actively engaging in the debates initiated by others.

The project video was produced and disseminated through both the project’s and all partners’ institutional channels.

A press release covering the 3rd Transnational Project Meeting in Bulgaria was prepared and shared by the hosting partner, Municipality of Smolyan.

RECinMED was also represented at the 2nd Gathering of the Green Living Areas Thematic Community of the Interreg Euro-MED Programme, held on 28–29 May 2025 in Barcelona, Spain, further strengthening its visibility within the programme community.

fr.name: Une multitude de communautés d'énergies renouvelables inclusives dans la région méditerranéenne
fr.description:
Une transition énergétique propre et juste ne peut reposer que sur l'implication directe de tous les acteurs agissant sur les territoires : citoyens, pouvoirs publics, entreprises privées et institutions scientifiques.
Une coopération plus forte au niveau transnational est nécessaire pour renforcer une approche intégrée afin d'innover les modèles actuels sur la base des meilleures pratiques et des études réalisées jusqu'à présent dans différents domaines.
RECinMED, à travers un partenariat composé de 11 partenaires de projet provenant de 9 pays et de trois partenaires associés, vise à tester des solutions innovantes afin de favoriser la création et le bon fonctionnement de communautés d'énergies renouvelables inclusives.
Le projet développera un nouveau forum de coopération et des activités de coordination au niveau transnational pour renforcer les réseaux et les retombées positives entre les projets concernant les communautés énergétiques.
En outre, trois groupes de travail seront créés afin d'analyser les connaissances existantes, de concevoir et de mettre en œuvre des actions pilotes dans trois contextes différents : les zones urbaines, les territoires ruraux et les îles.
Chaque groupe de travail sera confronté à des défis spécifiques et sera en mesure d'identifier la meilleure façon de transférer et de mettre à l'échelle des solutions validées dans de nouveaux domaines.
Des méthodologies et des outils déjà développés dans de précédents projets de l'UE ont été sélectionnés. Ils seront analysés et intégrés selon les meilleures pratiques et les données réelles disponibles pour les rendre plus efficaces et transférables dans des contextes géographiques présentant des caractéristiques et des besoins similaires.
Des rapports de test, des initiatives d'engagement des parties prenantes et un plan de transférabilité seront livrés avec une évaluation continue de la qualité à la fois pour les actions techniques et la gestion de projet, y compris la communication.
fr.expected_achievements:
Facilitate transnational cooperation among public and private stakeholders involved in the development of Renewable Energy Communities
This WP will be Coordinated by LP.
Test methodologies and tools for the development of RECs in different scenarios on a transnational scale.
This WP will be coordinated by PP2 UNIBO.
Provide validated information on the efficacy of methodologies, support schemes and tools for the development of RECs in Mediterranean islands, urban and rural areas, while defining a Transferability plan
